title: thymus serpyllum subsp. subayachicus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. subayachicus, also known as Creeping Thyme or Wild Thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, characterized by its low-growing, spreading habit and small, pink or purple flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum subsp. subayachicus include its ability to support respiratory health, alleviate stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Thymus serpyllum subsp. subayachicus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in cooking and as a natural insect repellent.
